verses.text: The LORD spake to Moses after the death of Aaron's two sons. To Aaron: come not at all times into the holy place within the vail before the mercy seat, lest die — for I will appear in the cloud upon the mercy seat. Guidance: The Day of Atonement begins with the memory of Nadab and Abihu. Access to God's presence is the costliest privilege. Aaron may enter only once a year, only on this day, only by blood.

The LORD spake to Moses after the death of Aaron's two sons. To Aaron: come not at all times into the holy place within the vail before the mercy seat, lest die — for I will appear in the cloud upon the mercy seat. Guidance: The Day of Atonement begins with the memory of Nadab and Abihu. Access to God's presence is the costliest privilege. Aaron may enter only once a year, only on this day, only by blood.

Aaron comes into the holy place with: a young bullock for sin offering, a ram for burnt. Put on holy linen coat, breeches, girdle, mitre — wash flesh first. From Israel he takes two kids for sin and one ram for burnt. Guidance: On this day Aaron lays off his golden ephod and puts on plain linen — a servant's clothing, not a king's. The high priest enters God's presence dressed like a slave. Christ would empty Himself the same way.

The high priest enters God's presence dressed like a slave. Christ would empty Himself the same way.

From Israel he takes two kids for sin and one ram for burnt. Guidance: On this day Aaron lays off his golden ephod and puts on plain linen — a servant's clothing, not a king's. The high priest enters God's presence dressed like a slave. Christ would empty Himself the same way.

Aaron offers the bullock, atones for himself and his house. Takes the two goats, sets them before the LORD. Casts lots: one for the LORD, one for the scapegoat. The LORD's goat — sin offering. The scapegoat — presented alive, let go into the wilderness. Guidance: Two goats, one fate split between them. One bleeds in the holy place; one walks alive out of the camp carrying sins. Together they picture what one Lamb of God would later do alone.

Aaron brings the bullock for his sin offering, kills it. Takes a censer of burning coals, sweet incense beaten small, within the vail. Puts incense on the fire — the cloud covers the mercy seat, that he die not. Sprinkles the bullock's blood on the mercy seat eastward; before it seven times. Guidance: Incense rises first — the priest enters wrapped in prayer-smoke so he can see God and live. Then blood touches the throne. Worship without intercession kills; intercession without blood doesn't atone.

Kills the goat for the people; brings blood within the vail, sprinkles on the mercy seat. Atones for the holy place because of Israel's uncleanness. Does so for the tabernacle in their midst. No one inside when he atones. Goes to the altar, atones for it; puts blood on horns, sprinkles seven times. Guidance: Every holy thing gets cleansed — mercy seat, tabernacle, altar. Sin's stain has touched everything; blood must reach every surface. Even the place where forgiveness is given itself needs forgiveness.

When done atoning — bring the live goat. Aaron lays both hands on its head, confesses Israel's iniquities, transgressions, sins — putting them on the goat's head. Send him by a fit man into the wilderness. The goat bears all iniquities to a land not inhabited. Guidance: Sins spoken aloud over a head, then carried out of sight. Hebrews 9 places Christ in both goats — His blood the sin offering, His body the bearer-away. He takes our sins where no one can find them.

Hebrews 9 places Christ in both goats — His blood the sin offering, His body the bearer-away. He takes our sins where no one can find them.

Aaron enters the tabernacle, puts off the linen, washes, puts on his garments, offers burnt offering. Burns the fat. He that let go the scapegoat — washes clothes, bathes, comes into camp. Bullock and goat whose blood was brought in — burnt outside the camp; he that burns them washes, bathes. Guidance: Even the man who released the scapegoat needs washing — touching what carried sins makes one unclean. The carcasses burn outside the camp, the same direction Christ would walk on Calvary.

A statute forever. Seventh month, tenth day — afflict your souls, do no work. The anointed priest atones — puts on the linen, holy garments. Atone for the sanctuary, tabernacle, altar, priests, people. Once a year. Aaron did as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Once a year, on the tenth of Tishri, everything pauses. Israel afflicts the soul — fasts — while the priest carries blood inside the vail. The cleanest day of the year is the saddest one. Yet the day ends with God's people forgiven.
